description: Address review issues: hostage-eligible figure asymmetry, Phoenix double-death fragile inference, and hostage death counting inconsistency
steps: 1. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, fix the fallback take-hostage path to also allow capturing monsters (non-daimyo-type). Change the find condition to include (f.type === 'monster' && f.monsterCardId && !['su-yurei', 'sp-fukurokuju'].includes(f.monsterCardId)).

2. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, fix the Phoenix double-death inference. Add a check that Phoenix figure is actually still in the province (not captured as hostage). Check finalProvince.figures for a figure with monsterCardId 'sp-phoenix' owned by the seppuku owner.
3. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, remove the battleDeathCount += 1 line from the take-hostage case in the fallback path since hostage capture is not a death.
4. In src/types/game.ts, add monsterCardId field to capturedHostage interface so Phoenix capture can be detected.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback take-hostage path can capture non-daimyo-type monsters
Phoenix double-death flag is only set if Phoenix figure is actually in the province (not captured)
Hostage capture does NOT increment battleDeathCount in the fallback path
npm run build succeeds without errors
findings: All three review issues fixed: (1) Fallback take-hostage path now captures monsters excluding daimyo-types (su-yurei, sp-fukurokuju) and returns monster to reserve on capture, (2) Phoenix double-death now checks finalProvince.figures for Phoenix presence before setting phoenixDiedInBattle, preventing phantom VP when Phoenix was captured as hostage, (3) Removed battleDeathCount += 1 from take-hostage since capture is not a death. Also added monsterCardId to capturedHostage interface for future tracking.
